Our Mission

Who We Are

The Vourvoura Society was created to support the village of Vourvoura, Greece, the local Greek Orthodox Archdiocese, and to participate in philanthropic activities in and around the Washington, D.C. area.

Vourvoura sits in Arcadia, Greece — in the northwestern part of the Parnon mountains of the Peloponesos, at 1,000 meters above sea level and 22 kilometers southeast of Tripoli.

Heritage

Notable People from Vourvoura

1934 — 2017

Sam Panopoulos

Inventor of the Hawaiian pizza, created in Chatham, Ontario. A proud son of Vourvoura whose culinary innovation became one of the most debated creations in pizza history.

1885 — 1972

Charalambos Zouras

An Olympian at the 1908 London Olympiad. Competed in the Javelin (placed 4th), Discus, and Tug of War events — carrying the spirit of ancient Greece onto the world stage.

Happy 50th to The Hellenic Center

We are excited for many more years of celebrating and growing the Hellenic Spirit together.

Vourvoura, Greece

Our Village

Community

Stories & News

Chronicles of our community, our village, and our Hellenic heritage.

Community June 2025

Celebrating 50 Years of The Hellenic Center

This year marks a landmark anniversary — fifty years of fostering Greek culture, faith, and community in the Washington, D.C. area. The Vourvoura Society is proud to have been part of this journey since the very beginning.

Continue Reading
Village News April 2025

Spring in Vourvoura: A Report from the Village

Every spring, the Parnon mountains come alive with wildflowers and pine. Members who visited this season report that restoration work on the village square is progressing beautifully.

Continue Reading
History February 2025

Remembering Charalambos Zouras: Vourvoura's Olympian

In 1908, a young man from a small mountain village in Arcadia traveled to London to compete on the world's greatest athletic stage. We revisit his remarkable story and 4th-place Javelin finish.

Continue Reading
Community December 2024

The Society's Annual Holiday Gathering

Members and families gathered this December for the Society's annual holiday celebration at The Hellenic Center, featuring traditional dishes, live music, and readings from the Society's 120-year history.

Continue Reading

About

Founded in 1904, the Vourvoura Society has served the Greek diaspora of Washington, D.C. for over 120 years.
